task_completion.description: The Segment Editor module is open and a segment named 'Ventricles' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Segment Editor' module in the left panel. A segment list contains an entry named 'Ventricles' (or similar).

task_completion.description: The segmentation overlay correctly targets the lateral ventricles (dark, butterfly-shaped structures in the center of the brain) without excessive noise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the slice views (Red/Yellow/Green windows), a colored overlay covers the dark central fluid-filled cavities. The overlay should not cover the black background outside the skull or the skull itself, indicating thresholding and cleanup (Islands/Erase) were performed.

task_completion.description: The Segment Statistics module has been opened to calculate volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Segment Statistics' module in the left panel.

task_completion.description: The statistics table displays a calculated volume for the ventricles within the valid range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Segment Statistics table is visible with a row for 'Ventricles'. The 'Volume' column shows a value between 5 and 80 mL (or 5,000 and 80,000 mm3).

task_completion.description: The measurement result is saved to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a text editor displaying 'ventricle_measurement.json' or a file explorer showing the file was created. The content should include 'volume_ml'.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Slicer modules (Segment Editor, Segment Statistics) rather than purely command-line scripts or text editors.

integrity.description: The volume recorded in the JSON file matches the volume displayed in the Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value in the text editor screenshot matches the value visible in the Segment Statistics table screenshot (accounting for unit conversion if necessary).

integrity.description: The agent did not copy a pre-existing solution file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv' moving a file from a hidden solution directory to the target path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image (MRHead) is a T1-weighted MRI where ventricles appear as dark, butterfly-shaped cavities in the center of the brain. Normal lateral ventricle volume is typically 10-50 mL (10,000 - 50,000 mm3). 3D Slicer often reports volume in cubic millimeters (mm3) or cubic centimeters (cm3/mL). 1 mL = 1000 mm3. A valid segmentation should result in a volume between 5 mL and 80 mL.
